behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—unit-testing only the prompt-builder can miss wiring bugs where the classifier omits labels before calling the client. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network is strictly required. MOCK-HIDE: a too-shallow mock at function level could hide missing labels in the actual module->client boundary. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier with the real internal API-client adapter but a local in-process fake transport that captures outbound payloads; call classify() and assert the transmitted label list exactly matches configured taxonomy (no missing/extra labels).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: no—failure boundary is local mapping/validation logic. REAL-INFRA: no. MOCK-HIDE: no, because a direct response fixture can exercise this branch. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Call the response-normalization/classification function with a simulated API result containing an out-of-taxonomy label and assert returned value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—mocks are deterministic and cannot reveal model output variance. REAL-INFRA: yes (real API call), but core risk is model non-determinism. MOCK-HIDE: yes, mocked responses always pass stability checks. LLM-DEP: yes—correctness depends on real model behavior across runs. MULTI-STEP: no, this is repeated single-step calls.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real API classifications for the same fixed document under production-like parameters and assert consistency threshold (e.g., 100% or agreed tolerance); fail if label drift occurs.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—local token estimators or mocked usage fields may not match provider-side metering. REAL-INFRA: yes—needs real API/token accounting behavior. MOCK-HIDE: yes, mocked token counts can trivially pass. LLM-DEP: limited; this is primarily metering/integration, not model judgment quality. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative documents through the real API path, read actual reported token usage per call, and assert each call is <= configured budget; include failure output with measured usage vs budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—unit tests of retry helper alone can miss wiring with classifier call path. REAL-INFRA: not strictly required; deterministic simulated 429/RateLimit responses suffice. MOCK-HIDE: shallow mocks could hide missing wait/retry orchestration, so test should include real retry/backoff components. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real classifier + retry/backoff stack with a controllable fake API client that returns rate-limit error then success; assert classify() eventually returns success and verify elapsed/fake-clock advanced by required wait interval(s).
